message:
refactor(runtime): migrate hot-path loops/channels to runtime_compat primitives

Scope:
This commit advances the asupersync/runtime-compat migration by removing direct tokio
time/channel/socket dependencies from several long-running loops and test harnesses.

Key runtime changes:
- Replaces tokio::time::interval-driven loops with explicit runtime_compat::sleep cadence in:
  - cpu_pressure monitor
  - memory_budget manager
  - memory_pressure monitor
  - survival model updater
  - telemetry collector
- Preserves immediate-first-iteration behavior using first_tick guards before sleep.

Channel/runtime abstraction updates:
- Swaps direct tokio::sync::mpsc imports for crate::runtime_compat::mpsc in:
  - ipc
  - native_events
  - tailer

Vendored unix socket test alignment:
- Converts test-only UnixListener binds in vendored mux client/pool modules to runtime_compat::unix::bind.
- Aligns async read/write test imports to runtime_compat unix traits where needed.

Workflow and distributed stream consistency:
- workflow wait utilities now use std::time::Instant where runtime semantics do not require tokio-specific clock types.
- distributed agent stream loop in frankenterm main now uses timeout-based heartbeat scheduling,
  reducing tight coupling to tokio interval semantics while preserving periodic snapshots.

Why this matters:
- Increases portability and consistency for upcoming runtime backends.
- Reduces direct tokio surface area in long-lived orchestration loops.
- Keeps behavior deterministic and easier to test under shared runtime abstractions.
